message:
chore(beads): refresh issue tracker timestamps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2796,7 +2796,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3681t.5.8","title":"[connectors] Multi-host connector mesh federation","description":"Integrate mesh capabilities for running connector workloads across multiple hosts/zones while preserving policy, observability, and failure containment.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Mesh routing and tenancy boundaries are explicit. 2. Cross-host behavior is resilient to partial outages. 3. Control-plane visibility remains consistent across hosts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OliveHarbor","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:13:43.720804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-04T04:42:33.597187Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T04:35:31.799664Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","mesh","multi-host"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:09.171426Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:13:43.720804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:08.568078Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1844,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","author":"OliveHarbor","text":"Claiming this bead. Will implement multi-host connector mesh federation module with host registration, zone routing, failure containment, and observability.","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:25:03Z"},{"id":1850,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","author":"OliveHarbor","text":"Implementation complete: connector_mesh.rs committed (5c199389). Types: ConnectorMesh, MeshHost, MeshZone, HostHealth, RoutingStrategy/Request/Decision, MeshFailureEvent, MeshHealthSnapshot, MeshTelemetry. Features: zone-aware routing (LeastLoaded/ZoneAffinity/RoundRobin), heartbeat timeout detection, failure containment, bounded history. 55+ unit tests. cargo check passes; test binary compilation blocked by system load (50+ concurrent cargo processes).","created_at":"2026-03-04T03:01:07Z"},{"id":1859,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"OliveHarbor: connector_mesh.rs committed (5c199389). cargo check --tests passes. Test execution blocked by aws-lc-sys native compile failure and USB_NVME stalls. 55 test cases written. Needs verification on healthier build env.","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:26:08Z"},{"id":1862,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"OliveHarbor: All 49 connector_mesh tests PASS. Root cause of prior test build failure: cc shell alias maps to Claude Code, not C compiler. Fix: CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang. Verified with clean build+test run.","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:42:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.5.9","title":"[connectors] Tier-1 connector bundles and audit-chain ingestion","description":"Deliver a high-value tier-1 connector set (e.g. GitHub, Slack, Linear, Discord, Gmail, Notion class) plus normalized audit-chain ingestion into FrankenTerm storage/search/policy systems.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Tier-1 connectors are productionized under lifecycle/security rules. 2. Connector activity is indexed for search and policy evidence. 3. Bundle quality and onboarding docs are sufficient for scale use.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via  {"id":"ft-3681t.6","title":"[epic] Unified policy, security, and governance","description":"# Epic 6: Unified Security, Policy, and Governance

Merge mux/orchestration controls and connector permissions into one enforceable governance model with approvals, revocation, and forensic traceability.","acceptance_criteria":"1. One policy model governs terminal actions, workflows, and connector capabilities.
2. Approval and revocation flows are enforceable and auditable.
3. Forensics/export cover operational and compliance needs.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:44.964150Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:32:00.690518Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","governance","policy","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:44.964150Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:50.565928Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:50.957244Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1723,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Design note: Security/governance is centralized to avoid split-brain decisions between terminal actions and connector actions. Unified policy + identity + approval + revocation is required for safe large-agent operations.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:57Z"}]}

Policy model covers all major action surfaces. 2. Decisions produce explainable traces. 3. Policy changes are testable and safely deployable.

Added PolicySurface enum + input/context plumbing in policy engine, exposed surfaces in PolicyRuleMatch config and specificity/catch-all logic, added surface match evaluation in rule matcher, and added/updated tests in policy + config + proptest_policy. Also fixed upstream compile blockers in resize_scheduler and hardened bench cfg in benches/tui_rendering for all-target checks. Validation via rch wrappers: workspace check/clippy/fmt are currently blocked by pre-existing unrelated workspace issues (frankenterm-gui unresolved k9/benchmarking in shapecache.rs; clippy cargo-common-metadata on tabout/window; widespread pre-existing fmt diffs). Focused gate cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets passes (warnings only).","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:00:37Z"},{"id":1835,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Progress update (HazyBarn, 2026-03-03/04): tightened policy surface attribution for mux mutation paths. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs, PolicyEngine::check_send compatibility shim and PolicyGatedInjector::inject now explicitly set PolicySurface::Mux on PolicyInput before authorize(). Added regressions: check_send_compatibility_path_uses_mux_surface and injector_policy_context_marks_mux_surface. Validation (required via rch wrappers): rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib (pass); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib check_send_compatibility_path_uses_mux_surface -- --nocapture (pass); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib injector_policy_context_marks_mux_surface -- --nocapture (pass). Note: rch remote SSH failed in these runs and fell back to local execution.","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:32:35Z"},{"id":1839,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Follow-up slice (HazyBarn): explicit policy surface tagging added in MCP tool handlers to increase cross-surface traceability. Updated crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs policy inputs with with_surface(...) mappings: ReadOutput/SearchOutput/SendText -> mux, WorkflowRun -> workflow, ReservePane/ReleasePane -> swarm. Added missing import crate::policy::PolicySurface. Validation (via rch wrappers): rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib (pass after import fix); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp_tools::tests::tool_count_is_29 -- --nocapture (pass). Remote worker SSH failed; rch failed open to local execution.","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:40:00Z"},{"id":1873,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"GoldHollow","text":"GoldHollow starting focused slice: finish explicit PolicySurface attribution in production CLI policy call sites in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs (read/search/send/prepare/commit paths). I have exclusive reservation on crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and will run targeted rch-offloaded validation before posting results.","created_at":"2026-03-04T06:39:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-3681t.6.3","title":"[policy] Approvals, revocation, quarantine, and kill-switch controls","description":"Implement high-confidence governance controls: approval workflows for sensitive actions, revocation flows, quarantine mode for compromised components, and emergency kill-switch semantics.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Sensitive actions are gateable with approvals. 2. Revocation/quarantine can be applied quickly and safely. 3. Emergency controls are tested for correctness under stress.

 {"id":"ft-39wn","title":"wa robot approve: submit approval codes in robot mode","description":"# wa robot approve

## Purpose
Allow agents to submit approval codes programmatically through robot mode. This mirrors the human `wa approve` command but with JSON output suitable for automation.

## Command Interface
```bash
# Submit an approval code
wa robot approve <code>

# Submit with explicit pane context (for fingerprint validation)
wa robot approve <code> --pane 3

# Check approval status without consuming (dry-run)
wa robot approve <code> --dry-run
```

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "command": "approve",
  "data": {
    "code": "a1b2c3",
    "valid": true,
    "action_kind": "SendText",
    "pane_id": 3,
    "expires_at": 1737300000000,
    "consumed_at": 1737296400000,
    "action_fingerprint": "sha256:abc123..."
  }
}
```

## Error Cases (Stable Codes)
- E_APPROVAL_NOT_FOUND: No approval with given code
- E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED: Approval code has expired
- E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED: Approval already used
- E_FINGERPRINT_MISMATCH: Action changed since approval issued
- E_WRONG_PANE: Approval was for different pane
- E_WRONG_WORKSPACE: Approval was for different workspace

## Implementation Notes
- Query ApprovalStore for the approval token
- Validate workspace scope, expiry, and fingerprint
- Mark approval as consumed on success (unless --dry-run)
- Record in audit trail

## Relationship to Human Command
- `wa approve` (wa-nu4.3.2.12): Human-friendly CLI with colored output
- `wa robot approve` (this bead): JSON output for agent consumption
- Both use the same underlying ApprovalStore

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_approve_valid_code() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_approval(...);
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code]);
    assert!(output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert!(output["data"]["valid"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert!(output["data"]["consumed_at"].is_number());
}

#[test]
fn test_approve_expired_code() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_expired_approval();
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code]);
    assert!(!output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED");
}

#[test]
fn test_approve_consumed_code() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_approval(...);
    store.consume(&code);
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code]);
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED");
}

#[test]
fn test_approve_wrong_pane() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_approval_for_pane(5);
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code, "--pane", "3"]);
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_WRONG_PANE");
}

#[test]
fn test_approve_dry_run_does_not_consume() {
    let store = setup_approval_store();
    let code = store.create_approval(...);
    
    execute_robot(&["approve", &code, "--dry-run"]);
    
    // Code should still be valid
    let output = execute_robot(&["approve", &code]);
    assert!(output["data"]["valid"].as_bool().unwrap());
}
```

### E2E Test
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_approval_flow.sh
set -euo pipefail
LOG="$ARTIFACT_DIR/approval_flow.log"
log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== Approval Flow E2E ==="

# 1. Try to send something that requires approval
log "Attempting send that requires approval..."
SEND_RESULT=$(wa robot send 0 "test" 2>&1 || true)
log "Send result: $SEND_RESULT"

# 2. Extract approval code from RequireApproval response
CODE=$(echo "$SEND_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.allow_once_code // empty')
if [ -z "$CODE" ]; then
    log "No approval code in response, skipping approval test"
    exit 0
fi

log "Got approval code: $CODE"

# 3. Check with dry-run first
log "Dry-run approve..."
DRY=$(wa robot approve "$CODE" --dry-run)
log "Dry-run result: $DRY"
echo "$DRY" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: dry-run"; exit 1; }

# 4. Actually approve
log "Approving..."
APPROVE=$(wa robot approve "$CODE")
log "Approve result: $APPROVE"
echo "$APPROVE" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: approve"; exit 1; }

# 5. Verify it was consumed
log "Verifying consumed..."
RETRY=$(wa robot approve "$CODE" 2>&1 || true)
log "Retry result: $RETRY"
echo "$RETRY" | jq -e '.error.code == "E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED"' || { log "FAIL: not consumed"; exit 1; }

log "=== PASS: approval_flow ==="
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Approval submission returns structured JSON
- [ ] All 6 error cases return stable error codes
- [ ] --dry-run flag works (doesn't consume)
- [ ] --pane flag for explicit context works
- [ ] Approval consumption is audited
- [ ] JSON validates against wa-robot-approve.json schema
- [ ] Unit tests pass for all error cases
- [ ] E2E test passes with detailed logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:51:09.929485182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:47:25.953597Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:47:25.953602Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-39wn","depends_on_id":"ft-4vx.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-39wn","depends_on_id":"ft-4vx.7.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-39wn","depends_on_id":"ft-4vx.8.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"ft-3axa","title":"Custom Allocator (jemalloc + Per-Pane Arenas)","description":"# Custom Allocator (jemalloc + Per-Pane Arenas)

## Goal
Replace FrankenTerm mux server default allocator with jemalloc + per-pane arena allocation to reduce fragmentation and enable per-pane memory tracking.

## Why jemalloc
- Industry-proven for long-running server processes (used by Firefox, Redis, Rust itself)
- Thread-local caches reduce contention under 200+ panes
- Predictable fragmentation behavior
- Built-in profiling (MALLOC_CONF=prof:true) for leak detection
- Works on both Linux and macOS (via tikv-jemallocator crate)

## Per-Pane Arena Allocation
Each pane gets a dedicated arena:
- Scrollback allocations go to pane-specific arena
- On pane close: entire arena is dropped (no leak possible)
- Arena stats: track per-pane memory usage without /proc parsing

```rust
use tikv_jemallocator::Jemalloc;

#[global_allocator]
static GLOBAL: Jemalloc = Jemalloc;

pub struct PaneArena {
    arena_index: u32,  // jemalloc arena index
    pane_id: PaneId,
}
```

## Memory tracking
- jemalloc mallctl API for arena-level stats (bytes allocated, bytes resident)
- Per-pane memory dashboard: RSS per pane without /proc dependency
- **Cross-platform**: jemalloc stats API works on both Linux and macOS

## Tests
- Verify jemalloc is the global allocator
- Per-pane arena allocation and deallocation
- Memory stats per arena match expected allocations
- **Criterion benchmarks**: allocation throughput with vs without arenas
- Stress test: 200 pane arenas created/destroyed rapidly, verify no leak
- Profile: compare fragmentation before/after (jemalloc prof)

## Acceptance criteria
- jemalloc as global allocator via tikv-jemallocator
- Per-pane arena allocation working
- Arena stats exposed for per-pane memory tracking
- Memory fragmentation measurably reduced under 200-pane load
- Works on Linux and macOS

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deliver scoped performance/architecture enhancement with no hidden feature loss or safety regression.
2. Require comprehensive validation: unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end scripts, and benchmark/proptest/formal checks as appropriate to the mechanism.
3. Require structured diagnostics/logging for critical runtime decisions, resource usage, and failure taxonomy with reproducible artifacts.
4. Heavy experimental/validation workloads must run via rch and include before/after evidence.
5. Closure requires explicit proof of measurable user/system benefit and residual-risk ownership.","notes":"CRITICAL: jemalloc #[global_allocator] requires unsafe. frankenterm-core uses #\![forbid(unsafe_code)]. RESOLUTION: Create a separate crate 'frankenterm-alloc' containing only the allocator setup (global_allocator attribute, jemalloc-sys bindings). Export per-pane arena allocation via a safe Arena<T> API. Feature-gate behind 'jemalloc' feature flag. The binary crate (frankenterm) selects the allocator, not frankenterm-core. Tests: allocation benchmarks in the alloc crate, memory pressure integration tests in frankenterm-core via safe API.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenStream","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:43:47.055141Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-28T06:34:32.085050Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3axa","depends_on_id":"ft-20fw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3axa","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3axa","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3axa","depends_on_id":"ft-ixt4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Code changes:
- crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml: add frankenterm-alloc dependency.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/ingest.rs:
  - PaneRegistry now owns PaneArenaRegistry.
  - discovery_tick reserves arena on new pane and releases on closed pane.
  - PaneEntry now stores pane_arena metadata.
  - added API: pane_arena, pane_arena_count, pane_arenas_snapshot.
  - added test discovery_tick_tracks_pane_arena_lifecycle.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs:
  - status payload now includes allocator (backend/jemalloc/stats or stats_error).
  - status payload now includes pane_arenas (active_count + pane/arena_id list; source=unavailable when no registry).
  - expanded ipc_status_returns_event_bus_stats assertions.
  - added test ipc_status_with_registry_reports_pane_arenas.

Validation (all via rch exec invocation path):
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features ✅ pass.
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features discovery_tick_tracks_pane_arena_lifecycle -- --nocapture ✅ pass.
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features ipc::tests::ipc_status_with_registry_reports_pane_arenas -- --nocapture ✅ pass.
